description: Normal-road lateral-crossing truck from the left at short TTC, but the usual right escape corridor is compromised by a pedestrian and a right-side blockage while a close follower makes hard braking costly.
hypothesis: When a left-crossing truck would normally trigger rightward drift, occupancy/blockage of the right escape side plus a close follower should move the boundary toward earlier braking or a constrained left/straight mitigation depending on TTC, lateral speed, and available right-side gap.
